description: The undergraduate concentration in Archaeology and the Ancient World provides students with an opportunity to explore the multi-faceted discipline of archaeology, and encourages an interdisciplinary approach to engaging with the ancient world. While the core focus is archaeology and art of the ancient Mediterranean, Egypt, and the Near East, this concentration encourages students to engage with Brown's many strengths in history, epigraphy, art, ethics, engineering, religious studies, and the sciences. The concentration has three distinct but overlapping tracks, allowing students flexibility in structuring their own path through this diverse field of study.

courses.name: Field Archaeology in the Ancient World

courses.name: Myriad Mediterraneans: Archaeology, Representation and Decolonization

groups.name: One ARCH course on a thematic or theoretical topic in archaeology

courses.name: Heritage In and Out of Context: Museum and Archaeological Heritage
Engineering and Technology in the Ancient World
Microarchaeology

courses.name: Buried History, Hidden Wonders: Discovering East Asian Archaeology
Archaeology Under the Volcano
Archaeology of the Andes

groups.name: Two additional ARCH courses at the 1000-level or above
notes: Examples: ANTH 1720, ARCH 1792, ARCH 1870; use these to define a specialty in archaeological theory, museum studies, ethics, materials analysis, etc.

groups.name: Two non-ARCH courses relating to the ancient world or the discipline of archaeology
notes: Chosen with DUS approval from 1000-level+ offerings in Anthropology, Classics, Egyptology and Assyriology, Environmental Studies, Geological Sciences, History, HIAA, Religious Studies, etc. One term of relevant language study may count.
